Video.summary: This episode examines the features of late medieval culture in Italy that paved the way for the Renaissance.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: Italy and the Renaissance

Video.summary: Around 1400, study a European-wide style known as International Gothic flourished in Italy.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: From Gothic to Renaissance

Video.summary: This episode traces Donatello's work until he moved to Padua in 1443.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: Donatello and Luca della Robbia

Video.summary: This episode looks at the buildings of Filippo Brunelleschi and the famous bronze doors of Lorenzo Ghiberti.
Video.grandparentTitle: Great Artists of the Italian Renaissance
Video.title: Brunelleschi and Ghiberti in Florence

Video.summary: Examine the visual nature of Roman society and the role of spectacle.
Video.grandparentTitle: Experiencing Rome: A Visual Exploration of Antiquity's Greatest Empire
Video.title: Rome-A Spectacular Civilization

Video.summary: Learn to understand the visual components of the Roman Empire.
Video.grandparentTitle: Experiencing Rome: A Visual Exploration of Antiquity's Greatest Empire
Video.title: A Brief Survey of the Roman Empire

Image.alt: A Brief Survey of the Roman Empire
A Brief Survey of the Roman Empire
A Brief Survey of the Roman Empire

Video.summary: Learn the role of Rome\'s military in projecting images of power.
Video.grandparentTitle: Experiencing Rome: A Visual Exploration of Antiquity's Greatest Empire
Video.title: Power, Conquest, and Romanization

Video.summary: Conquest led to triumphal processions, sacrifices, and other spectacles.
Video.grandparentTitle: Experiencing Rome: A Visual Exploration of Antiquity's Greatest Empire
Video.title: Triumphal Processions-Victory Parades

Video.summary: Delve into the history of the chiefdoms that dominated the American South before the arrival of Europeans.
Video.grandparentTitle: A New History of the American South
Video.title: The Geography of the American South

Image.alt: The Geography of the American South
The Geography of the American South
The Geography of the American South

Video.summary: Learn how the Atlantic slave trade was initiated by the Portuguese.
Video.grandparentTitle: A New History of the American South
Video.title: The World of Slavery

Video.summary: Trace the rise of slavery in the British Caribbean.
Video.grandparentTitle: A New History of the American South
Video.title: Slavery Becomes American

Video.summary: Witness how Barbados planters spurred the colonization of the Carolinas as a slave-based rice economy.
Video.grandparentTitle: A New History of the American South
Video.title: The Southern Colonies Take Root

Video.summary: Look at Franklin's unfinished autobiography, in which he created a persona for others to follow.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Age of Benjamin Franklin
Video.title: Benjamin Franklin and the American Dream

Image.alt: Benjamin Franklin and the American Dream
Benjamin Franklin and the American Dream
Benjamin Franklin and the American Dream

Video.summary: Meet Franklin's parents and siblings, learn the story of his wife and first-born son, and more.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Age of Benjamin Franklin
Video.title: Meet the Franklins

Video.summary: Delve into the Boston of the time to witness the events Franklin experienced in his youth.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Age of Benjamin Franklin
Video.title: Benjamin Franklin's Boston Beginnings

Video.summary: Discover how the city of Philadelphia shaped Franklin'and how Franklin shaped the city.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Age of Benjamin Franklin
Video.title: Benjamin Franklin and Philadelphia

Video.summary: How did the American West come into being, and how was it shaped by myth?
Video.grandparentTitle: The American West: History, Myth, and Legacy
Video.title: Westward the Course of Empire

Video.summary: To understand the American West, you have to understand the mark left by its earliest colonists.
Video.grandparentTitle: The American West: History, Myth, and Legacy
Video.title: The West in the Colonial Era

Image.alt: The West in the Colonial Era
The West in the Colonial Era
The West in the Colonial Era

Video.summary: After the Revolutionary War, how was land between the Appalachians and the Mississippi organized?
Video.grandparentTitle: The American West: History, Myth, and Legacy
Video.title: Venturing beyond the Appalachians

Video.summary: Follow the fascinating journey of the two explorers who mapped the Louisiana Purchase.
Video.grandparentTitle: The American West: History, Myth, and Legacy
Video.title: Discoveries of Lewis and Clark

Video.summary: Begin your study of federalism with a look at the 2000 presidential election dispute between George W. Bush and Al Gore. Examine the historical and philosophical foundations of federalism.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Evolution of American Federalism
Video.title: Federalism: America's Great Innovation

Video.summary: Step back to the founding of America to explore the rise of our two-party political system. Professor Hoffmann walks you through six phases of party politics.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Evolution of American Federalism
Video.title: How Federalism Shapes American Politics

Video.summary: Throughout American history, federal and state governments have clashed over interpretations of the law and states' rights. Survey this rocky relationship across time.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Evolution of American Federalism
Video.title: States Can't Nullify Federal Law

Video.summary: Although federal law reigns supreme, the Constitution outlines a limited domain for federal authority. Here, you will learn about the federal power of taxing and spending.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Evolution of American Federalism
Video.title: The Federal Government Can Tax and Spend

Image.alt: The Federal Government Can Tax and Spend
The Federal Government Can Tax and Spend
The Federal Government Can Tax and Spend
